reflection.quote: The most regretful people on earth are those who felt the call to creative work, who felt their own creative power restive and uprising, and gave to it neither power nor time.
reflection.reading:
Sundays used to be the worst day of the week. Either I was hungover and bargaining with the ceiling, or I was already pre-loading the next round to outrun the dread of Monday. The day had no shape. It was a holding pen between damage and obligation.

These days, Sunday is mine. I make coffee that I actually taste. I go for a walk without a clock on it. Sometimes I'm productive and sometimes I read on the couch for four hours and let that be enough. The radical thing isn't the activity — it's that I'm here for it. I remember it on Monday. The day belongs to me, not to whatever I drank the night before.

Mary Oliver was talking about creative work, but I think the principle is wider than that. Whatever is restive and uprising in you — the friendships you've been meaning to tend, the body you've been meaning to move, the quiet you've been meaning to sit in — it needs power and time. Sobriety is what gave me back the hours. A Sunday with nothing scheduled used to feel like a threat. Now it feels like a gift I almost didn't get to open. The small ordinary day, fully shown up for, is not a consolation prize. It might be the whole point.
reflection.meditation: What would I do today if I trusted that this day, exactly as it is, belongs to me?

affirmations: I am awake for my own life today, and that is not a small thing.
Today I give my time to what actually matters to me, not to what's loudest.
I am building a Sunday — and a life — I don't need to escape from.

gratitude.prayer: I'm grateful for a quiet morning that I can remember. Grateful for the people who picked up the phone when I was unbearable, and for the ones who are still around now that I'm not. Grateful for a body that walks, lungs that work, and a mind clear enough to notice the light coming through the window. None of this was guaranteed, and I haven't forgotten that.
gratitude.closing: One more day on the right side of it. That's the whole prayer.
